title: End-to-end trip-planner tutorial 08 in repos/docs (trip-planner Round 7 — cap-stone)
description: Write the cap-stone tutorial for the Adiona/muno trip-planner project at `repos/docs/docs/tutorials/08-trip-planner-end-to-end.md`. Walks the reader through a complete trip-planning session in muno against the GKE NoETL backend. Uses 8 captured screenshots from Kadyapam's local demo run at `/Volumes/X10/projects/adiona/tutorial_screenshots/`. References execution IDs as proofs from prior rounds. Includes a Firestore replay walkthrough using `ai-meta/scripts/firestore_replay.sh`. Explicit 'What's NOT covered' section pointing at post-tutorial roadmap (real-money booking, mobile responsive, multi-user auth, ICS export, Google Calendar push). Cap: 1 docs PR (`repos/docs`). Pre-handoff: NONE — screenshots already captured. Scoping reference: `sync/issues/2026-05-12-trip-planner-app-scoping.md` Round 7 section.

pre_handoff_for_kadyapam.status: DONE. Screenshots captured at /Volumes/X10/projects/adiona/tutorial_screenshots/. No new GCP setup. No new secrets.
screenshot_inventory: 01-chat-start.png — 3-column shell visible; bot greeting + user 'Find flights to Paris'; PlaceAutocompleteInput with 'Paris'; Eiffel Tower place card; DateRangePicker (07/01/2026 → 07/05/2026, 4 nights). Right pane shows Trip state with Destination: Paris.
02-places-card.png — Scrolled view showing the full Eiffel Tower place card (landmark tag, 120000 reviews, Add to itinerary CTA) + the first flight card emerging.
03-date-picker.png — Closer view of DateRangePicker + the flight list with 2 UA SFO→JFK 199 USD offers, Watch In Detail + Book This CTAs.
04-flight-search.png — Flight cards in detail + Hotels section header with 'Relax filters for more options' info banner.

05-picked-flight.png — Order confirmation card 'Booking confirmed / Reference ABC123 / USD 199.00 / Alex (passenger)' with View full order + New search CTAs. Hotels showing Central Hotel (8.7/10, 4 stars, 'Rooms matching: 3', USD 180).
06-hotel-search.png — Hotel card + Schedule section emerging (Jul 15 2026 8AM Depart SFO + Jul 15 2026 12PM Hotel check-in events with edit pencils).
07-calendar-view.png — Full Schedule (CalendarView widget) + Itinerary Summary card (Paris, 2026-07-01 to 2026-07-05, Party JSON, Draft itinerary, USD 1200 total, Confirm + Edit CTAs).
08-itinerary-summary.png — Same Schedule + Itinerary Summary visible PLUS the input bar at thread bottom ('Tell Adiona where you want to go' placeholder + Send button).
known_v1_polish_nits_to_flag_honestly_in: Party renders as raw JSON `{"adults":2}` instead of 'Party: 2 adults' — v1 formatting nit.
Place card photo placeholder is grey — Google Places photo URLs not yet wired through the widget renderer.
Hotel card photo placeholder is grey — same as above for Amadeus hotels.

Booking reference 'ABC123' is a synthetic placeholder rendered by the agent — actual Duffel test booking refs (like 'XKQAYC' from round 20260512-220000) are stored in Firestore but not yet displayed verbatim in the OrderConfirmation widget.
Schedule shows 'Jul 15, 2026' while date picker shows '07/01/2026 → 07/05/2026' — captured across multiple demo runs; date consistency is a v1 polish item.

Trip state right pane shows only Destination + Dates in this capture; the full slot accumulator (party, star rating, budget, etc.) is wired but collapsed in the screenshot — note the design supports a richer panel that future rounds will surface fully.

design_brief_for_codex.tutorial_audience: Developers and product folks who've already read tutorial 07 (`07-travel-agent-with-widgets.md`) and want to see the end-to-end trip-planner story — how the LLM-driven agent, Firestore event sourcing, Duffel orders, Amadeus hotels, Google Places, and the muno chat UI fit together to plan a real trip. Reading level: assumes familiarity with NoETL playbooks and the prior travel agent tutorial; does NOT assume Firebase or React knowledge.
design_brief_for_codex.tutorial_structure:
Approximately 7 sections totaling ~1000-1400 lines of markdown:

  ## Vision (intro, ~60 lines)
     - One-paragraph 'what we built and why'.
     - Diagram (mermaid): user → muno chat UI → NoETL itinerary-planner agent → mcp/{firestore,duffel,google-places,amadeus} → Duffel/Amadeus/Google APIs.
     - 'Rounds 1-7' summary table with status + key link per round (pull from the memory files at `ai-meta/memory/inbox/2026/05/*-green.md`).
     - Embedded screenshot 01-chat-start.png as the hero image.

  ## Prerequisites (~80 lines)
     - GCP project (noetl-demo-19700101) with: Firestore Native, mcp/{firestore,duffel,google-places,amadeus} playbooks registered, secrets (duffel-api-test, google-maps-widget-key) provisioned.
     - GKE cluster `noetl-cluster` in us-central1 with WI annotation on noetl-worker-mcp.
     - muno repo cloned + `npm install` ready. VITE_GOOGLE_MAPS_KEY env var.
     - Reference Pattern C setup playbook + the Duffel/Firestore round memories.

  ## Walkthrough (~600 lines, 8 numbered sub-sections — one per screenshot)
     1. **Starting the chat** (uses 01-chat-start.png). Reader runs `kubectl port-forward` + `npm run dev`. Sees the 3-column shell. Notes the Guest mode + the Trip state right pane.
     2. **Free-form chat → place card** (uses 02-places-card.png). User types 'Find flights to Paris'. Agent's LLM extraction step pulls `slot_updates.region`. Tool dispatch to `mcp/google-places.search_text`. Renders a `place_list` containing the Eiffel Tower card.
     3. **Date-range picker → date selection** (uses 03-date-picker.png). Agent emits a `date_range_picker` widget. User submits 07/01/2026 → 07/05/2026. Event: `user_widget_submit` → agent applies `slot_updates.check_in_date` + `check_out_date`. Right pane updates.
     4. **Flight search** (uses 04-flight-search.png). Agent dispatches `mcp/duffel.search_offers` with SFO→JFK (or CDG depending on inferred origin). Results emit as `flight_list` containing `flight_card.compact` items. Show the 'Watch In Detail' + 'Book This' CTAs.
     5. **Book a flight (test-env order)** (uses 05-picked-flight.png). User clicks 'Book This'. Agent dispatches `mcp/duffel.create_order` with synthetic passenger + balance payment. Receives a real Duffel test booking reference. Emits `order_confirmation` widget showing the booking confirmed state. Hotels start loading underneath.
     6. **Hotel search via Amadeus** (uses 06-hotel-search.png). Agent dispatches `mcp/amadeus.search_hotels`. Surfaces 'Relax filters for more options' upsell banner. Shows Central Hotel card. Notes the friendly-failure path if Amadeus 500-flakes — covered in `sync/issues/2026-05-11-amadeus-test-api-500s.md`.
     7. **Schedule populates via Firestore** (uses 07-calendar-view.png). After flight order + hotel pick, the agent writes calendar event documents to `users/{uid}/trips/{tripId}/events/` via `mcp/firestore.set_doc`. The `CalendarView` widget subscribes via Firebase JS SDK `onSnapshot` and rerenders. Show the schedule: Flight departs SFO + Hotel check-in.
     8. **Itinerary summary + Confirm** (uses 08-itinerary-summary.png). Agent emits `itinerary_summary` with the picked flight + picked hotel + total cost. User clicks Confirm → agent finalizes the trip in Firestore. Input bar visible at the bottom for free-form chat (e.g. 'add a 3pm meeting on Wednesday' → user_note calendar event).

  ## Behind the scenes — replay the session (~100 lines)
     - Show `ai-meta/scripts/firestore_replay.sh thread-list --limit 5` to find the demo thread.
     - Run `firestore_replay.sh events chat_threads/{thread_id} --from 1 --to 30` to walk the event log.
     - Output snippet showing user_message → agent_slot_update → agent_tool_call → agent_tool_response → agent_widget_emit chain.
     - Reference `events/api_calls/` for full request/response audit (with header redaction confirmed).
     - Note that replay is deterministic for slot updates + widget sequence; LLM chat text drift is tolerated.

  ## What's NOT covered in v1 (~80 lines, roadmap section)
     - Real-money booking (Thread 3 — payment + monetization decisions: PCI scope, processor choice, KYC).
     - Mobile responsive (Figma has `mob-*` variants; deferred to a Round 6c).
     - Multi-user auth (Firebase Auth — current state is Guest only).
     - Google Calendar sync (Kadyapam locked to Firestore-only; documented in `muno/docs/architecture/calendar-design.md`).
     - ICS export (post-tutorial).
     - Place + hotel photo wiring (placeholders in v1).
     - Party display polish ('2 adults' instead of raw JSON).
     - Real booking reference display.
     - Right-pane property block full slot surfacing.
     - Filter narrowing widgets (star rating slider, budget range, amenities checkbox group) — wired in schema but agent doesn't yet emit them.

  ## Try it yourself (~80 lines)
     - Clone muno + dependencies.
     - Run `kubectl -n noetl port-forward svc/noetl 8082:8082` (matches the prior tutorials' pattern).
     - `VITE_GOOGLE_MAPS_KEY=$(gcloud secrets versions access latest --secret=google-maps-widget-key --project=noetl-demo-19700101) npm run dev`.
     - Open http://localhost:5173.
     - Type 'Find flights to Paris' and walk the flow.
     - Inspect Firestore via the console OR via `firestore_replay.sh`.

  ## Related (~40 lines)
     - All 7 rounds' memory entries with brief one-liners.
     - The scoping doc.
     - Pattern C setup playbook.
     - Figma source path (the design that drove the UI).
design_brief_for_codex.screenshot_handling: Copy all 8 PNGs from `/Volumes/X10/projects/adiona/tutorial_screenshots/` into `repos/docs/static/img/tutorial-08/` (or wherever the Docusaurus/MkDocs/etc. static image dir is — INSPECT repos/docs structure first to determine the right location). Reference them from the markdown with relative paths per the docs site's convention. Optimize each PNG via `optipng -o 5` or `pngquant` if those binaries are available, otherwise commit as-is.

Pull authentic execution IDs from the result JSONs in `bridge/outbox/`:
  - Pattern C SA OAuth probe: 200 (from round 20260512-030000, but a probe not an execution ID).
  - Duffel test order: 625452830463099795 (booking ref XKQAYC, from round 20260512-220000).
  - Default-Duffel flights smoke: 625385246619337115 (from round 20260512-130000).
  - Amadeus override smoke (friendly-failure path proof): 625309687340073612 (from round 20260512-130000).
  - Firestore append_event smoke: capture from round 20260512-235000 result JSON.
  - Itinerary agent end-to-end smoke from round 20260513-010000 result JSON.
  - Calendar view live smoke: 625590045860168070 (from round 20260513-020000).

Use these as inline proofs (e.g., 'A real test order produced booking reference XKQAYC; you can replay the full request/response audit via execution 625452830463099795').
design_brief_for_codex.what_NOT_to_do:
- DO NOT claim production-mode capability. Everything in v1 is test-env: Duffel test wallet, Amadeus test API. Tutorial must say so explicitly in the Vision section.
  - DO NOT show full Duffel test tokens, GCP service-account JSONs, Figma PATs, or any other credential bytes in code blocks. All examples use `<...>` placeholders.
  - DO NOT recommend deploying the v1 permissive Firestore rules to a real production project. The tutorial's deployment section explicitly notes the rules are demo-only.
  - DO NOT promise feature parity with commercial trip planners. The tutorial frames muno as a reference implementation + technical demo, not a competitor to Kayak/Hopper/Booking.com.
  - DO NOT update tutorial 07's content. This is purely additive (a new 08).
  - DO NOT modify muno or any other repo. The tutorial is entirely in repos/docs.
design_brief_for_codex.docs_site_inspection: Before writing, INSPECT `repos/docs/` to determine: docs framework (likely Docusaurus or MkDocs Material), where existing tutorials 01-07 live, where static images go, the markdown frontmatter convention (e.g., `--- title: ... sidebar_position: 8 ---`), and how cross-tutorial links are formatted. Mirror the conventions exactly. The tutorial must build and render cleanly when the docs site is built locally.

1_inspect_docs_repo_structure.objective: Determine the docs framework + conventions to mirror.
spec: `ls repos/docs/` — note the framework files (docusaurus.config.js / mkdocs.yml / etc).
`ls repos/docs/docs/tutorials/` — confirm tutorial 07 exists and note its frontmatter shape.
`head -20 repos/docs/docs/tutorials/07-travel-agent-with-widgets.md` — capture the frontmatter convention.
Find where static images live: likely `repos/docs/static/img/` for Docusaurus or `repos/docs/docs/assets/` for MkDocs. Capture the path.

Run the docs build locally if possible (e.g., `npm run build` or `mkdocs build`) to confirm baseline GREEN — if not possible without extra deps, document the manual verification step in the result JSON.
1_inspect_docs_repo_structure.verify: Framework identified; image path chosen; tutorial 07 frontmatter captured.

2_copy_and_optimize_screenshots.objective: Stage the 8 PNGs in the docs static-images directory.
spec: Create `repos/docs/static/img/tutorial-08/` (or equivalent based on phase 1).
Copy all 8 PNGs from `/Volumes/X10/projects/adiona/tutorial_screenshots/` into that directory, preserving filenames (`01-chat-start.png` etc.).
Optimize via `optipng -o 5` or `pngquant --quality=85-95 --skip-if-larger` if available. If not, commit as-is.
Record original + optimized sizes in the result JSON.
2_copy_and_optimize_screenshots.verify: 8 PNGs land in the right directory; each is readable; total weight is reasonable (under ~5 MB combined target).
